paper_title: Analysis of Habitat Quality and Phytocoenoses Changes

methods.description: This method assesses changes in habitat quality over time using historical forest survey data. It involves comparing tree heights to standard tables to determine habitat quality.

steps.description: Changes in the habitat were based on data from forest surveys.
The quality was determined by comparing the height of trees with the average height of a standard pine of the same age listed in timber tables.
steps.searchable_summary: compare tree heights to standard pine tables

steps.description: A transition matrix and a diagram showing changes in the habitat quality classes were prepared for each site based on the collected data.
steps.searchable_summary: create transition matrix and diagram for habitat quality changes

methods.description: This method analyzes changes in the syntaxonomy of plant communities using relevés data and numerical ordination techniques.

steps.description: Relevés from 1961, 2002, and 2013 were used in the analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: collect relevés data from specified years

steps.description: The relevés were collated in a phytosociological table based on a chronological sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: organize relevés into a chronological phytosociological table

steps.description: Numerical ordination of relevés was performed using PCA.
Ellipses showing 95% confidence intervals for relevés were marked on the PCA ordination diagram.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize PCA results with confidence intervals

steps.description: A transition matrix of different types of communities in three development stages was prepared.
steps.searchable_summary: create transition matrix for community types

methods.description: This method monitors modern pollen deposition using pollen traps and analyzes the data to calculate pollen accumulation rates.

steps.description: Pollen traps installed at three sites are included into network of Pollen Monitoring Programme.
steps.searchable_summary: install and monitor pollen traps at specified sites

steps.description: Traps stayed in the ground all year round and their content was treated in a laboratory using standard methods.
steps.searchable_summary: process pollen trap contents using standard lab methods

steps.description: The percentage values and pollen accumulation rates of individual pollen taxa were calculated for each year.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen accumulation rates and percentages
